name: simple_model
positioning: A repo-native Project Intelligence control plane that can also be consumed as a Codex skill/plugin: easy to install, easy to invoke from arbitrary repos, deterministic in CI, and useful to maintainers who did not author simple_model.

implementation_audit.summary: The v0.5 Project Intelligence core and Codex skill/plugin exist and are published, but the plugin experience is still a developer-grade wrapper. The next roadmap should harden installation, invocation, packaging, release automation, cross-repo use, diagnostics, examples, and telemetry-free adoption UX.
current_strengths: A repo-local Codex skill exists at codex/skills/simple-model-project-intelligence.
A plugin package exists at plugins/simple-model-project-intelligence with the skill bundled.
The README documents clone, marketplace add, and plugin install commands.
The plugin release path now has a deterministic v0.6 packager and version policy.
The skill wrapper can call validate, ingest, audit, interface scan, facts, PR gate, dashboard, and resolve commands.

major_gaps.gap: Install instructions are documented but not self-tested in CI or by an install smoke script. Users may hit Codex CLI version differences without guidance.
The plugin duplicates the skill directory, so source skill and bundled plugin skill can drift. There is no sync check or release packager.
The wrapper assumes it can find the simple_model root, but usage from an arbitrary target repository is awkward and does not support a configured toolchain path.
There is no doctor command that verifies bash, jq, Codex CLI, plugin registration, writable output dirs, and target repo readiness.
The skill explains commands, but the wrapper does not expose structured command metadata, examples, or next-step suggestions.
Plugin zips and GitHub releases were created manually. There is no deterministic release script that validates, packages, tags, and publishes assets.
There is no small external fixture repo showing clone -> plugin install -> ingest -> PR gate end to end.
README has installation commands, but lacks troubleshooting, version compatibility, uninstall/update flows, and the expected new-thread boundary after plugin install.

implementation_audit.planning_principle: Treat the plugin as a product surface, not only a packaging artifact. Every install, invocation, and release path should be deterministic, scripted, and testable.

plugin_readiness: A new user can install the plugin from a fresh clone by following one README section.
A smoke test verifies marketplace metadata, plugin manifest, bundled skill, wrapper help, and at least one wrapper command.
Source skill and plugin-bundled skill cannot drift without a failing test.
The wrapper can run from either the simple_model repo or a separate target repo with an explicit SIMPLE_MODEL_HOME.
A release script can validate, package, tag, and upload the plugin zip repeatably.
quality_bar: Every new command has --help output and stable exit codes.
Every plugin/skill metadata file is validated in tests.
No global Codex config is modified by tests.
Documentation covers install, update, uninstall, troubleshooting, and release assets.
The plugin remains telemetry-free and repo-local by default.

todos.task: Add a deterministic smoke test for the repo-local Codex plugin package.
todos.current_gap: Plugin validation was run manually with a temporary PyYAML environment and is not part of the test suite.
acceptance: Validates .agents/plugins/marketplace.json with jq
Validates plugins/simple-model-project-intelligence/.codex-plugin/plugin.json with jq
Validates bundled skill frontmatter and agents/openai.yaml
Asserts wrapper help exits 0
Asserts no TODO placeholders remain in plugin/skill files

todos.task: Prevent drift between the source skill and the plugin-bundled skill.
todos.deliverable: A sync script and test that compare file hashes or refresh the bundled copy deterministically.
acceptance: A check reports exact files that differ
A sync mode refreshes the plugin-bundled skill from codex/skills
The test suite fails if the two skill copies drift
Generated caches and non-skill files are ignored

todos.task: Make simple_model_pi.sh ergonomic from arbitrary target repositories.
todos.current_gap: The wrapper mainly assumes execution from the simple_model repo and does not clearly separate toolchain root from target repo.
todos.deliverable: Cross-repo wrapper mode with SIMPLE_MODEL_HOME and --target-root support.
acceptance: SIMPLE_MODEL_HOME can point to the simple_model checkout
--target-root selects the repository being analyzed
Wrapper commands work when CWD is outside simple_model
Error messages explain how to set SIMPLE_MODEL_HOME
Existing repo-root usage remains backward compatible

todos.task: Add a doctor command for plugin and skill readiness diagnostics.
todos.current_gap: Users have no single command to diagnose missing jq, old bash, missing Codex CLI, bad marketplace registration, or invalid target repo paths.
acceptance: Checks bash version, jq, git, and optional gh/codex availability
Checks simple_model root discovery
Checks plugin manifest and marketplace JSON parse
Checks target repo path when provided
Emits JSON mode for machine-readable diagnostics
Exits non-zero only for hard blockers

todos.task: Expose wrapper command metadata for discoverability and future MCP/tool generation.
todos.current_gap: The wrapper has help text but no structured command catalog with descriptions, args, outputs, and examples.
acceptance: Command manifest lists validate, full-check, ingest, audit, interfaces, facts, pr-gate, dashboard, resolve, and doctor
Each command declares required args, optional args, output type, and example invocation
simple_model_pi.sh commands --json emits the manifest
README references the command discovery path

todos.task: Add a minimal external target repo fixture for end-to-end plugin usage.
todos.current_gap: Existing tests prove generators, but not the user journey of using the plugin against a separate half-built repo.
acceptance: Fixture contains a small Python or TypeScript app with public interfaces and tests
Plugin wrapper can ingest the fixture
Plugin wrapper can scan interfaces for the fixture
Plugin wrapper can run a PR gate against fixture changes
Fixture remains small enough for fast tests

todos.task: Create a one-command demo for the plugin user journey.
todos.current_gap: Users must mentally stitch together install, ingest, audit, interface scan, and PR gate steps.
acceptance: Runs without modifying global Codex config
Uses the target repo fixture
Shows ingest, audit, interface scan, facts, dashboard, and PR gate
Emits a concise summary with output paths
Has a test smoke path

todos.task: Upgrade README plugin documentation into a complete install/update/uninstall/troubleshooting guide.
todos.current_gap: README now shows install commands but lacks compatibility, update, uninstall, troubleshooting, and release asset guidance.
acceptance: Documents clone-based install
Documents release zip usage
Documents update/reinstall flow after new releases
Documents uninstall or marketplace removal path where supported
Documents starting a new Codex thread after install/update
Documents common failures and doctor command usage

todos.task: Add a deterministic release packager for the Codex plugin.
todos.current_gap: The original v0.5.0 plugin zip was created manually; v0.6 adds a deterministic packager with sync/validation steps.
acceptance: Runs skill/plugin sync check first
Validates plugin manifest and skill metadata
Creates dist/simple-model-project-intelligence-plugin-<version>.zip
Includes only plugin package files, not generated build artifacts
Prints sha256 checksum

todos.task: Add CI coverage for plugin package readiness and release packaging.
todos.current_gap: Plugin validation and packaging are not enforced by GitHub Actions.
todos.deliverable: GitHub Actions workflow or generator output that validates plugin package on PRs.
acceptance: CI runs plugin package smoke test
CI validates marketplace JSON and plugin manifest
CI checks source/bundled skill sync
CI can run package script without publishing
Workflow avoids modifying global Codex config

todos.task: Bridge the command manifest to MCP/tool schemas for richer Codex tool invocation.
todos.current_gap: The project has an MCP wrapper, but the plugin does not expose command schemas or a clear route from command manifest to MCP tools.
todos.deliverable: Generated MCP tool schemas derived from the command manifest.
acceptance: MCP tools/list includes plugin commands with schemas
MCP tools/call can invoke read-only plugin commands
Errors are structured and include remediation hints
No write/publish command is exposed through MCP by default
Tests cover initialize, tools/list, and one tools/call path

todos.task: Define and enforce version alignment between repo release, plugin manifest, marketplace entry, and release asset names.
todos.current_gap: The plugin manifest now follows a documented plugin version policy, but future releases must continue enforcing that alignment.
acceptance: Documents whether plugin version follows repo version or independent semver
Checks release asset name against selected version policy
Checks plugin manifest version in package script
README displays current plugin version source of truth
Release notes template includes plugin version
